blocks.content: Chapter 14: First Step Toward the Dream (6)
Kite untied the practice sword from his waist and handed it to Rian.
As the real sword approached, Rian's heart pounded.
Though he didn't know what he was supposed to do with it, the intention behind it was clear.
It was a message: if you want to save your friend, you must be prepared to draw your sword.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: If you can enter the mansion with this sword, I'll send you off. But if you're not prepared for that much, don't move from here. It's already out of your hands.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Master.
A hot smile appeared on Rian's lips as he looked at Kite.
He bowed respectfully and extended both hands, and Kite silently handed over the sword.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Thank you. I won't forget this kindness.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Remember this. Abandoning your friend is abandoning another part of yourself.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Yes!
Rian fastened the sword to his waist and ran toward the mansion.
Kite watched the scene with satisfaction.
This was the end.
The time for farewell had come a little early, but he was glad they could part ways with a manly spirit.
May good fortune follow his disciple's future.
A chilling atmosphere filled the mansion.
Work had stopped, and the butlers and maids stood in place without making a sound.
Behind the sofa where the direct family members sat, Head Butler Louis adjusted his glasses.
Next to him, Sub-Butler Temuran glared at Shirone with a stern gaze.
Shirone knelt before the direct family members, enduring countless hostile and contemptuous stares.
It felt like he had stepped into the middle of a colosseum.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Head Butler, report.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Yes, Head of the Family. Temuran hired Shirone, who has resided here for a year and six months, and is now accused of disrespecting the authority of the Ozent family. Many servants have witnessed Shirone associating with the youngest young master, and I myself have heard Shirone speaking casually to him. The detailed evidence will be attached in the testimony documents prepared by Temuran.
Bishop silently moved his hand behind his back.
As he received the documents Louis handed him, he flipped through them roughly with sharp, deliberate motions.
While his father read the documents, Reina quietly approached Shirone and whispered in his ear.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Shirone, don't worry too much. I'll try to persuade Dad. Nothing will happen.
Shirone slowly raised his head, his face eerily calm.
It was so unsettling that it sent shivers down Reina's spine.
Even in this situation, there wasn't a trace of fear in his expression.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: He's not pretending to be calm. He genuinely isn't afraid.
Shirone wasn't afraid. His mind was currently floating outside the cliff. The future was nothing more than an illusion that didn't exist yet. Nothing had been decided.
After skimming through the documents, Bishop spoke.
With his pale complexion befitting the black-haired lineage, his voice carried a chilling aura.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Arian Shirone, is the content of these documents true?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Dad, Shirone and Rian—

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Reina, I wasn't asking you. I'll give him a chance to speak. Stay quiet for now.
Reina thought it wouldn't be easy.
Bishop, who based his authority on principles and cold rationality, wasn't someone who could be persuaded by emotions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's one thing I'd like to ask.
The abnormal composure surprised the watching servants, but it had no effect on Bishop.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Speak.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If I and the youngest young master are friends, what happens to him?
Even in a life-threatening situation, Shirone's first concern was his friend.
Reina's face softened affectionately at his kindness.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: It won't affect him at all. This gathering is to interrogate you, a commoner. No matter what crime Rian has committed, as long as it's a family matter, we will protect and shield him.
Bishop's words were laced with thorns.
It was a warning not to think that a noble's son would be treated the same as a commoner.
But Shirone was satisfied with that.
Shedding the last of his worries, Shirone smiled brightly and readily admitted the truth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then that's a relief. It's true that Rian and I are friends. We've built a good friendship over the past year and will continue to do so.
No one showed it outwardly, but Shirone's words caused a huge stir.
Especially for the servants who had lived with him, the shock was immense when something they had doubted turned out to be true.
A noble family's child and a hunter's son had been friends for a year.
It was the kind of thing you'd only see in a play, yet it had actually happened.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: The family's authority has fallen to the ground. Shirone, as the head of the Ozent family, I will execute you.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Dad! Wait, listen to me! You said you'd give him a chance to speak!

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: He confessed himself. Dragging this out further is pointless. Head Butler.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Yes, Head of the Family.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Carry out Shirone's execution. And this matter must never leak outside.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Understood.
Reina grew frantic.
She was almost resentful of Shirone for remaining so calm in this situation.
But how could she stop it?
Her father had never once reversed a decision he made as the family head.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Who dares to execute him without my permission!
Rian burst into the mansion, kicking the door open as if to break it.
Just his entrance alone seemed to raise the temperature in the mansion.
Everyone knew Rian's temperament, and that was precisely the power of fire he possessed.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Who's trying to execute my friend without my permission?

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: I am.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Why are you doing this? He hasn't committed a capital crime—he just became friends with me!

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: You don't need to commit a capital crime to die. A noble's circumstances are complicated. It's because we handle those complicated circumstances that you've grown up enjoying privileges as a noble's son.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If those privileges come at the cost of killing a friend, then I refuse them!

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: How naive, Rian. A friend is someone you share mutual feelings with on equal footing. Do you think that child considers you a friend? How can you believe that? For example, what if he approached you for money? Even then, you wouldn't know. And yet, you still believe in him?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone isn't the kind of person to do something so low!

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: That's exactly what I'm asking. How can you believe that? Show me proof that Shirone is that kind of person.
Reina, who had been listening to the conversation, bit her lip.
Rian was being dragged into Bishop's rhetoric.
There was no proof that Shirone was a true friend.
Whether he had approached with ulterior motives or genuine friendship, Shirone's attitude wouldn't have shown any difference.
Rian also felt a chill at his father's logic.
It was cold.
Asking for proof of friendship.
It was so cold it felt like he might freeze.
Rian gripped the real sword at his waist.
His master's heat seemed to transfer through his hand.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hahahaha!
And then he laughed heartily.
He had finally understood his master's true intention.
The sword hadn't been given to him to throw a tantrum.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Dad, you're really something. I almost fell for it. Proof? I believe in him. Even if Shirone doesn't consider me a friend, as long as I believe in him, Shirone is my friend. That's the clearest proof there is!
This time, Bishop grew heated.
After all, Rian's words were also true.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: So you're defying the family head's orders? This isn't just a matter between father and son. If you disobey the family head's command, you could be disowned.
Bishop had drawn a line in the sand.
Disowning his own son.
The butlers, and even the direct family members, couldn't help but be taken aback by this statement.
But Rian didn't even blink.
As if facing a superior officer rather than family, he expressed his will with unprecedented formality.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If disownment is the price I must pay to protect my friend, I'll gladly accept it with a smile.
Bang!
Bishop stomped his foot on the floor.
It was unacceptable.
His 17-year-old son was willing to be disowned?
It was a tantrum.
A disrespectful act.
He was exploiting the weakness of the parent-child relationship to play hardball.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Draw your sword. If you're willing to be disowned, it means you're ready to abandon even your family, right? If you truly stand for your friend, stake your life on it.
Rian drew his sword.
That action only angered Bishop further.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Are you really pointing a sword at your own father?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: This sword isn't meant to cut you down. It's a sword to protect my beliefs.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Since when have you cherished friends? You didn't even mingle with noble peers. Because they shone too brightly. You're using a commoner inferior to you as a sacrifice to comfort yourself.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: No. This time, you're wrong, Dad. Shirone is a friend who shines brighter than me. I want to help Shirone shine even more.
Bishop was dumbfounded.
What had that child done?
What kind of words had he used to bewitch his simple-minded son into completely submitting?

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Was it you? Did you convince Rian to become like this? Did you persuade him that nobles and commoners can be friends?
Even as the family teetered on the brink of disaster, Shirone remained calm.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. I told him that.
Rian stepped in front of Shirone and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone, stay back. I'll handle this. No matter what happens, I won't let you—

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rian.
The cold tone, unlike anything Rian had heard before, made him turn around with a bewildered expression.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You must take responsibility for what's yours to take.
His voice, tone, demeanor, and intent—everything was as cold as Bishop's, if not more so.
Even Rian felt the chill, let alone the family.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, yeah. Of course.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But...
Shirone continued.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You don't need to take responsibility for what isn't yours. We've always done everything together to get this far. This time, we'll overcome it together too.
As if it had never happened, Shirone's face softened warmly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if it means death, I don't regret becoming friends with you.
Shirone's final words exploded in Rian's already burning heart.
Tears welled up.
At this point, he could no longer see clearly.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: That's it! Even if I'm cast out, we're friends!
The butlers began to murmur.
Even those who had initially sneered now couldn't decide whose side to take.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: How impressive, Shirone.
Reina, who had been watching Shirone with her chin propped on her hand, smiled.
With a single strict word, he had cowed the adults in the room.
Moreover, he had made Rian's fiery temper burn even brighter.
In a way, he might be the most similar in personality to their father.
Yet, the reason Shirone still shone was that his words were filled with sincerity, not calculated strategy.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Bwahaha! This is a masterpiece! To think I'd get this excited in my later years! Youth really is the best!
The laughter was so loud it seemed to shake the mansion, making the maids scream.
In contrast, the direct family members sighed and turned around upon hearing the voice.
A blue-haired old man with a buzz cut stood on the second-floor balcony.
Shirone stared blankly.
Even if you called him Rian's final evolved form, it wouldn't be an exaggeration.
His massive frame and bulging arm muscles seemed ready to burst.
It was Ozent Clump, Rian's grandfather.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Grandfather!
Reina stood up with the expression of someone who had just received divine reinforcements.
She and Rian alone hadn't been enough to persuade the black-haired family head, but with their grandfather here, things would be different.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: You've arrived, Father. Reina said it would take a few more days.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Kehehe! My friend ran off with a young bride, so I came early. But this place is as lively as ever. Youth really is something.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: This isn't a good thing. The family's dignity has fallen to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Is that so? From what I've been watching, everyone seems to have a lot to say. Why don't we hear everyone's opinions before deciding whether our dignity has fallen or not?
Reina cheered inwardly, 'Grandfather is the best!'
Though Clump had already passed the family head position to his son, his status as a publicly recognized third-grade swordsman was enough to earn the family's respect.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Then I'll speak first.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: I only met Shirone yesterday, but he was clever and talented. Also, as a contract worker, he fulfilled all his duties and was supposed to leave the family today. Given these circumstances, it's hard to believe Shirone had any ill intentions toward Rian, and thus, I don't think he has disgraced the family's honor.
Reina gave a short but firm opinion and sat down.
But the tide hadn't turned yet.
At best, the scales had balanced.
Though the blue-haired bond was stronger than the black-haired one, Clump, who had once been the family head, was fair-minded.
He gave the opposing side, Rai, a chance to speak as well.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Then what does our second grandson think?

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: I dislike that child.
The butlers began to murmur.
This was none other than the family's most promising member speaking.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: But I don't want to bother with him. It's none of my business.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: What do you mean? You dislike him but don't want to bother with him?

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: It's exactly as I said. I don't like that child. But I don't want to lie about my feelings just because I dislike him. I said I don't want to bother with him because I don't want to bother with him. No matter who Rian befriends, I just want to end this matter quickly and go to the royal palace.
Reina clicked her tongue.
Even if he was her younger brother, his self-centeredness ran deep.
But this time, his personality might work in Rian's favor.
Clump chuckled like a boy.
He enjoyed seeing each family member's unique personality.
Moreover, this was precisely the strength of the Ozent family, with its two mixed bloodlines.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Then lastly, let's hear the opinion of the sub-butler who has observed Shirone the longest.
Temuran stepped forward.
Louis gave him a sidelong glance, signaling him to choose his words carefully.
The family could say whatever they wanted, but for a butler, the most important person was his employer, Bishop.

blocks.speaker: Temuran
blocks.content: Shirone has perfectly carried out all the tasks I assigned him over the past year and six months.
Louis's brow furrowed unpleasantly.
All he had to do was fabricate one sentence.
This was why he could never rise above being a sub-butler.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Did you know they were friends?

blocks.speaker: Temuran
blocks.content: I knew the youngest young master often went out with him, but I didn't know they were friends. But in my humble opinion, if he had been a reckless child without discernment, he wouldn't have been able to organize ten thousand books.
Shirone's eyes widened in surprise.
He had never dreamed Temuran thought of him that way.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: So you're taking Shirone's side now?
Temuran, seasoned as a butler, bowed with an obsequious smile.

blocks.speaker: Temuran
blocks.content: Not at all. I, too, was shocked that a contract worker like Shirone had befriended the youngest young master. I am merely stating the facts as a sub-butler.
With this, the verdict was effectively over.
Bishop was the only one still insisting on punishing Shirone.
No matter how much authority the family head had, he couldn't go against the united voice of the family.
Rian, his tension easing, let out a deep breath.
Shirone also felt relieved and smiled at Rian.
But not everything was over yet.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: I cannot accept this.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: Father.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Grandfather!
Bishop and Rian called out to Clump simultaneously, but the emotions in their voices were polar opposites.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: I understand that the two of you acknowledge each other as friends. But that's as far as it goes. If this relationship continues, rumors will spread, and other nobles might make a meal of it. So, Rian and Shirone, think of this as a good memory and end your friendship here.
Reina was disappointed in her grandfather but had no rebuttal.
Family matters were important.
Saving Shirone's life was already a good outcome.
Clump met Rian's fiery gaze with a cheerful smile.
He knew how much his grandson adored him, but Rian also had a duty to protect the family's honor.
The fact that no one objected to this decision proved it.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Shirone, what do you say? I'm grateful you taught my foolish grandson about friendship, but can you let him go now? It's hard for nobles and commoners to mingle as friends.
Shirone, who had been staring at the marble floor, spoke.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can't do that.
The unexpected bold answer made Bishop, Reina, Rai, and even Clump raise their eyebrows in surprise.
But no one was more shocked than Rian.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shi, Shirone...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If I, a commoner, am an obstacle in Rian's path, I don't mind never seeing him again. If you still doubt me, you can even kill me. But that doesn't mean we weren't friends. Because Rian and I are friends.
Tears streamed down Rian's face.
They were tears of shame.
A part of him had been relieved just that Shirone wouldn't die.
In the end, it was he who had lacked trust.
Finally making up his mind, Rian raised his sword and shouted.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I hereby declare!
The family's expressions darkened with unease.
This wasn't like Shirone's case.
Whenever Rian got this excited, unexpected disasters always followed.
Their unease was spot on.
Rian turned to Shirone.
And before he could utter a word, he reversed his grip on the sword and plunged it into the floor in a flash.
The long sword pierced through the marble floor.
Shirone stared blankly at his reflection in the blade.
At that moment, Rian knelt on one knee and bowed his head to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ozent Rian of the Ozent family, from this moment onward, shall become Shirone's sword.

blocks.speaker: Bishop
blocks.content: What is this, you fool!
Bishop forgot his dignity and stood up, pointing angrily.
Rian's actions were utterly absurd.
Even Reina, who had been on Rian's side, looked incredulous.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Are you... making a knight's vow?
A knight's vow.
Nobles pledge loyalty to the royal family in exchange for stipends, but if you go back in time, royalty itself was once just nobility.
Thus, royal authority could always be overthrown, and a knight's loyalty was limited to the stipends and honor granted by royalty.
But a knight's vow was different.
It was a decision to reduce oneself to a mere sword, a pledge to become the sword of the lord one would serve for life.
A noble who defies the king is just a rebel.
But a knight who breaks a knight's vow is not only stripped of humanity but is also recorded in history as a stain on their family for generations.
Once in a lifetime.
Rian had just made that weighty pledge to Shirone.
Bishop's blood boiled.
Once a knight's vow was made, it couldn't be undone.
Even without emphasizing the gravity of the vow, breaking it would undoubtedly brand him as the world's biggest fool.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rian, what are you doing?
But Rian couldn't raise his head out of guilt.
He had wavered for a moment.
Compared to Shirone, who had staked his life to protect the name of friendship, all he had done was talk.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What do you think? Now there's no going back, right? We're in this to the end.
Compared to Shirone's resolve, a knight's vow was nothing.
Rian looked around at his family with a satisfied expression, sword in hand.
He had never seen such faces before.
It was a huge mess, but it felt exhilarating.
Clump stroked his massive chin, lost in thought.
Honestly, he hadn't expected such foolishness.
But he didn't feel displeased—perhaps because Rian took after him.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Well, you have to be this foolish to be truly foolish. Hehehe.
Burying his mixed feelings deep, Clump asked solemnly.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Rian, if you're willing to become his sword, does that mean that boy is worthy of it?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: More than worthy.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Then answer me. Is the standard of that worthiness the boy's greatness or your own inadequacy?
Rian pondered.
Was Shirone exceptional?
Or was he lacking?
No matter what conclusion he reached, it wouldn't satisfy the Ozents.
Why had his grandfather asked such a question?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: A sword does not judge. It only protects.
Clump was satisfied.
As Rian said, a sword does not think.
It only needs to cut through in the sharpest way its master desires.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: You have a way with words, for someone so lacking in swordsmanship. I pity your friend for getting a sword like you.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Grandfather!
Rian glared and shouted.
But the tension from before was gone.
Partly because he had already taken an irreversible path, but mostly because everyone was too exhausted to care.
Clump, true to his straightforward personality, wrapped things up cleanly.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Enough. This ends here. Help your master up. Shirone, Rian is still an unpolished sword, but he won't cut his master's feet. Take good care of my grandson from now on.
Shirone bowed silently.
Humility here would only hurt the Ozent family's pride.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He is a friend far beyond my worth. Thank you for allowing it.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: That boy did it on his own. What can I do? I heard your contract has ended. You were planning to return home, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. My parents must be waiting.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: You must miss them. But now that you two have pledged to be friends for life, it would be a shame to send you back like this. Hey, Head Butler.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Yes, Elder.

blocks.speaker: Ozent Clump
blocks.content: Send the carriage Shirone was supposed to take back now. Bring his parents here.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Understood.
The head butler left the mansion immediately after answering.
Whether it was the family head or anyone else, this matter was now far beyond his hands.
Louis once again realized how unfathomable noble thinking was.
Shirone chatted with Rian in his room.
From being summoned in the morning and made to kneel, it was now noon.
It felt like too much had happened in less than half a day.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Kehehe, did you see our family's faces earlier? It was so satisfying.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: By the way, Rian, are you really okay with this? That knight's vow thing. I feel like you might have gone too far for my sake.
Rian's playful expression turned serious.
Even as foolish as he was, Rian understood the weight of a knight's vow.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone, I didn't make that vow just because we're friends. You're different from my brother. You have a magnetism that draws people in. I've entrusted my life to someone like that.
Shirone felt Rian's sincerity.
But he still hated awkwardness.
Rian thought the only way forward was to deploy the underwear strategy again.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Alright. I'll make the suggestion. But the condition will probably be that you enroll in the swordsmanship school. Dad exploits weaknesses thoroughly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Tch! Whatever. I'll think about it.
Rian knew his father's personality all too well.
For Shirone's sake, he could easily enroll in the swordsmanship school.
Come to think of it, he needed it for himself too.
He had lost to a magic aspirant with a sword.
At this rate, he'd become a burden to Shirone rather than his sword.
Someone knocked on the door, interrupting their conversation.
A butler nervously opened the door.

blocks.speaker: Butler
blocks.content: Young Master Shirone's family has just arrived. They are to be brought to the mansion, but the family head ordered me to inform you in case you wish to greet them.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Oh! Shirone's parents? What do you want to do, Shirone?
It was a needless question.
Shirone's eyes were already fixed on the mansion's unseen front gate.
He was practically bouncing in his seat, ready to run out immediately.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Hehe. Go greet them, Shirone. We'll wait in the parlor. Escort Shirone, please.

blocks.speaker: Butler
blocks.content: Understood, Miss.
Shirone followed the butler out of the mansion.
The butler's attitude, which until yesterday had been "Hey, you!" had changed completely, much to Shirone's discomfort.
He seemed overly worried about Shirone tripping on a pebble.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Um, you can act like usual. It's not like I've become a noble or anything.

blocks.speaker: Butler
blocks.content: Not at all! How could I be so rude to the master of the youngest young master? Please don't say such things. My head will roll.
Shirone, who had once staked his life on a single book, understood that feeling better than anyone.
But that was precisely why he didn't want such treatment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I won't tell anyone even if you make a mistake, so please don't do this.
The butler nodded repeatedly, moved.
A commoner becoming a noble's master.
To the servants, Shirone was nothing short of a hero from legend.
At any rate, Shirone didn't pay it any more mind.
No, he couldn't.
At the end of the path symbolizing Ozent, a carriage had just arrived.
Two people stepped out.
At that moment, Shirone's vision blurred.
His parents, whom he hadn't seen in a year and six months, were there.
Memories of enduring hardships and staying up all night in the library flashed through his mind, and tears of sorrow poured out.
His legs moved on their own, driven by overwhelming emotion.
Not even hearing the butler's calls, Shirone ran toward the mansion's iron gates.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mom! Dad!
